CREATE TABLE test_data (id INT, value DOUBLE);
CREATE TABLE additional_data (id INT, value DOUBLE);
SELECT sys.var_pop(a.value) FROM test_data a JOIN additional_data b ON a.id = b.id;
SELECT sys.var_pop((SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE id = 1)) AS variance;
SELECT sys.var_pop(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT sys.var_pop(SELECT value + 5 FROM test_data) AS variance_plus_five;
SELECT sys.var_pop((SELECT AVG(value) FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL)) AS variance_of_average;
SELECT sys.var_pop(value) FROM (SELECT value FROM test_data WHERE value IS NOT NULL) AS filtered;
